- 2026-05-07 asset integrity remediation-severity UI follow-up: the browser Admin Asset Integrity metric grid now surfaces error-grade integrity remediation count from the existing bounded integrity remediation queue beside healthy state, action reasons, total remediations, status breadth, scanned assets, and failure totals, so operators can distinguish blocking storage-integrity work without opening remediation rows.
- 2026-05-07 remediation warning-count UI follow-up: the browser Admin Auth, Asset Storage, Asset Integrity, Rendering Operations, Rules Operations, AI Operations, and Plugin Operations metric grids now surface warning-grade remediation counts from their existing bounded remediation queues beside total and hard-blocker counts, so operators can distinguish cleanup backlog from critical/error production blockers without opening remediation rows.
- 2026-05-07 AI rejected-proposal recency follow-up: `GET /api/v1/admin/ai/operations` now includes a bounded redacted `proposalReview.recentRejected` queue, and the browser Admin AI Operations panel surfaces recent rejected proposal count plus recent rejected proposal rows beside pending, approved, stale, and apply-failure proposal telemetry, so operators can audit proposal-review churn without exposing raw proposal diffs.
- 2026-05-07 AI applied-proposal recency follow-up: `GET /api/v1/admin/ai/operations` now includes a bounded redacted `proposalReview.recentApplied` queue, and the browser Admin AI Operations panel surfaces recent applied proposal count plus recent applied proposal rows beside pending, approved, rejected, stale, and apply-failure proposal telemetry, so operators can audit completed AI proposal-review outcomes without exposing raw proposal diffs.
- 2026-05-07 plugin review recency follow-up: `GET /api/v1/admin/plugins/operations` now includes bounded approved and rejected review samples beside the existing pending and blocked review samples, and the browser Admin Plugin Operations panel surfaces recent approved/rejected review counts plus compact rows, so operators can audit marketplace moderation outcomes without opening the full review table.
- 2026-05-13 v1 gap-closure pass: campaign and scene creation in the browser now use setup forms for name, description, rules system, visibility, scene dimensions, grid size, and player activation instead of only demo defaults. Scene activation is exclusive on the API, selected-token authoring now exposes name, actor link, image asset, size, position, visibility, lock state, disposition, and deletion from the Actor panel, and journal/chat creation now expose visibility/type/body inputs instead of hardcoded `New Secret` and plain public chat. Combat gained `PATCH|DELETE /api/v1/combats/{combatId}` plus browser next/previous/end, round advancement, initiative editing, and defeated toggles. The reusable `@open-tabletop/api-client` now covers the public campaign, scene, token, journal, chat, combat, proposal, AI, plugin, system, content-import, and import/export surfaces while intentionally leaving server-admin/SCIM operations to operational tooling.
- 2026-05-13 auth setup posture follow-up: `GET /api/v1/admin/auth/operations` now includes redacted SCIM runtime posture with bearer-token presence, SCIM endpoint paths, provisioned user/group counts, and mapping/matched-mapping counts. The browser Admin tab now has an explicit Auth Setup section that presents OIDC required-variable readiness, invalid/insecure OIDC config names, SCIM token readiness, SCIM endpoint paths, and SCIM inventory counts without exposing provider URLs, client secrets, or bearer tokens.
- 2026-05-13 scene organization follow-up: scenes now accept optional `folder` metadata on create/update, duplicated scenes preserve the folder, and the browser scene setup/manager exposes folder assignment plus a scene-folder filter in the top scene switcher. The seeded Playwright tabletop path verifies editing a scene folder, filtering to that folder, and returning to all scenes before duplicate/reorder/delete coverage.
- 2026-05-13 destructive delete audit follow-up: campaign and scene delete routes now append redacted `campaign.delete` and `scene.delete` audit rows after owned-record cleanup, preserving deleted object identity and aggregate impact counts without storing deleted chat bodies or token names. Browser campaign and scene danger zones now preview the affected record counts and state that the delete is audited; the seeded tabletop E2E asserts the scene delete completion status includes audit logging.
- 2026-05-07 rendering coverage denominator UI follow-up: the browser Admin Rendering Operations metric grid now surfaces the feature-coverage scene denominator from the existing rendering feature coverage rollup beside production feature scenes and coverage percentages, so operators can interpret advanced fog, lighting, terrain, and vision readiness against the actual coverage scope.
- 2026-05-07 plugin approved-review UI follow-up: the browser Admin Plugin Operations metric grid now surfaces approved review count from the existing plugin review operations rollup beside review coverage, pending, rejected, blocked, and oldest-review metrics, so operators can scan marketplace package approval posture without opening review queue cards.

- 2026-05-11 AI failed-tool-retry-rate UI follow-up: the browser Admin AI Operations metric grid now surfaces retryable and non-retryable failed-tool rates from the existing failed-tool retry policy counters beside failed-tool counts, retry replay controls, replay success/failure totals, provider health, evaluation coverage, safety posture, and proposal review metrics, so operators can scan whether failed AI tool calls are mostly replayable or blocked without calculating the classification ratios manually.
- 2026-05-11 AI proposal-status-rate UI follow-up: the browser Admin AI Operations metric grid now surfaces pending, approved, applied, and rejected proposal rates from the existing proposal status counters and total proposal counter beside proposal status counts, recent proposal review samples, stale review posture, apply failure rate, failed-tool retry rates, provider health, evaluation coverage, and safety posture, so operators can scan AI proposal review flow distribution without calculating status ratios manually.
- 2026-05-11 AI proposal-review-risk-rate UI follow-up: the browser Admin AI Operations metric grid now surfaces approval-required, apply-ready, stale-pending, stale-approved, and total stale proposal rates from the existing proposal review counters beside proposal status counts and rates, stale review counts, oldest pending/approved ages, apply failure rate, failed-tool retry rates, provider health, evaluation coverage, and safety posture, so operators can scan AI proposal review backlog risk without calculating readiness and staleness ratios manually.
- 2026-05-11 AI proposal-source-entity-rate UI follow-up: the browser Admin AI Operations metric grid now surfaces tool, manual, scene, token, and actor proposal rates from the existing proposal source/entity counters and total proposal counter beside proposal source/entity counts, proposal status and review-risk rates, stale review posture, apply failure rate, failed-tool retry rates, provider health, evaluation coverage, and safety posture, so operators can scan AI proposal origin and target mix without calculating distribution ratios manually.
- 2026-05-11 AI tool-catalog-safety-rate UI follow-up: the browser Admin AI Operations metric grid now surfaces safe, unsafe, proposal-gated, fail-closed, strict-schema, and loose-schema tool rates from the existing AI tool catalog counters beside tool catalog counts, fail-closed gaps, proposal review rates, failed-tool retry rates, provider health, evaluation coverage, and safety posture, so operators can scan AI tool permission and schema hardening coverage without calculating catalog ratios manually.
- 2026-05-13 first-run owner bootstrap: empty API state now exposes `GET|POST /api/v1/auth/bootstrap`, creates the first password owner as a persisted server admin, creates a starter private campaign plus active scene, and blocks repeated bootstrap attempts. Durable stores can disable demo seeding with `OTTE_DEMO_SEED=false` or `seedDemo: false`; production defaults to clean state unless `OTTE_DEMO_SEED=true`. The browser checks bootstrap status before demo login and renders an owner setup screen when setup is required.
- 2026-05-13 browser account-flow follow-up: the browser no longer auto-loads the demo GM when no bearer session token is present. It renders an explicit login/register screen with optional SSO, reset entry, and deliberate demo-login action. Authenticated sessions expose Account rail controls for logout, password change, and TOTP MFA enroll/confirm/disable, with recovery codes shown after confirmation.
- 2026-05-13 invite-acceptance browser follow-up: unauthenticated `/join?invite=...` now renders a public Accept Invite form with the token prefilled, creates the invited password account, clears the unauthenticated state, and lands the player directly in the campaign. The Playwright suite now creates an invite as Demo GM and accepts it from a separate browser context.
- 2026-05-13 owner-bootstrap E2E follow-up: `pnpm e2e` now runs both the seeded campaign Playwright config and `playwright.bootstrap.config.ts`, which starts isolated API/web servers on separate ports with `OTTE_DEMO_SEED=false`. The bootstrap spec verifies a clean deployment routes to Create Owner, creates the first owner account and starter campaign, lands in that campaign, and exposes Admin navigation.
- 2026-05-13 campaign/scene management follow-up: the browser now exposes selected campaign settings and a selected scene manager for edit, activate, duplicate, reorder, and typed-confirm delete flows. Campaign delete now cascades campaign-owned child records, while scene delete removes scene-local tokens, chat, source fog presets, and token references from encounters and combats.
- 2026-05-13 browser E2E follow-up: the repo now has `pnpm e2e` backed by Playwright, isolated managed API/web servers on separate test ports, a fresh seeded SQLite test store, a clean no-demo bootstrap store, and Chromium smoke coverage for owner bootstrap, the explicit sign-in screen, demo GM login, campaign settings, scene manager edit/duplicate/reorder/delete, token toolbar, dice, chat controls, combat tracker start/edit/advance/end lifecycle, AI encounter proposal draft plus approve/apply review, AI memory extraction approval, AI proposal rejection, SDK plugin install/command and system roll/encounter planning flows, first-owner server-admin operations dashboard loading, GM/player realtime chat and whisper delivery across independent browser contexts, asset upload plus folder/tag metadata editing and scene background assignment, campaign archive export/re-import conflict safety, private-browser invite acceptance, and phone/tablet viewport reachability.
- 2026-05-13 scene reorder follow-up: `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/scenes` now returns scenes by `sortOrder`, new scene defaults are campaign-local instead of global across all campaigns, and the browser Scene Manager exposes Move Up/Move Down controls that swap sort order through the existing permissioned scene update route.
- 2026-05-13 asset metadata follow-up: `MapAsset` now supports optional `folder` and `tags`, uploaded assets can receive that metadata from browser upload controls, `PATCH /api/v1/assets/{assetId}` updates asset name/folder/tags behind the existing `scene.update` permission, and the asset library searches, filters by folder, and renders folder/tag metadata beside lifecycle and storage posture.
- 2026-05-13 GM/player realtime E2E follow-up: the invite acceptance Playwright test now keeps the Demo GM and invited player pages open in separate browser contexts, sends a GM public chat message and verifies the player sees it via realtime refresh, then sends a player whisper to the GM and verifies the GM sees it without a manual page reload.
- 2026-05-13 combat tracker E2E follow-up: the seeded Playwright suite now starts combat from scene tokens, verifies the active combatant appears, edits initiative, marks a combatant defeated, advances and rewinds round state, and ends combat back to the start state.
- 2026-05-13 AI proposal browser follow-up: the AI prompt textarea now has an accessible `AI prompt` label, and the seeded Playwright suite now drafts an encounter proposal from the browser, verifies the pending review state and prompt summary, approves/applies it, and verifies the proposal reaches `applied` with no repeat apply action.
- 2026-05-13 SDK/plugin/system E2E follow-up: the seeded Playwright suite now opens the Runtime SDK tab, installs the packaged Example Macro Plugin, runs `/spark` through the browser and verifies the resulting chat message, then exercises active D&D system encounter planning and the Strength Check roll action.
- 2026-05-13 admin operations E2E follow-up: the clean-bootstrap Playwright suite now verifies the first owner server-admin session exposes the Admin tab, loads the Server Admin panel to `Admin operations synced`, and renders Auth operations, Admin users, Admin AI operations, and Plugin marketplace review regions.
- 2026-05-13 campaign archive browser follow-up: the seeded Playwright suite now downloads a campaign archive from the browser Export action, saves the `.ottx.json` artifact to a temp path, imports it through the hidden archive input, and verifies the UI reports conflict-safe re-import status instead of silently applying a partial duplicate import.
- 2026-05-13 asset-library follow-up: the browser Content tab now exposes a searchable campaign asset library with image thumbnails, lifecycle/security/storage metadata, upload and upload-as-background controls, scene background assignment, signed delivery URL generation, and active/archive/delete lifecycle actions. The Playwright smoke now uploads an SVG asset through the library, verifies the new asset card, and assigns it as the active scene background.
- 2026-05-13 mobile/tablet E2E smoke follow-up: the seeded Playwright suite now runs phone-sized and tablet-sized viewport smoke tests that sign in as Demo GM and verify the tabletop toolbar, Chat tab, Content tab, and Actor rail remain reachable.
- 2026-05-13 mobile/tablet workspace-order follow-up: the phone/tablet layout now orders the live tabletop workspace before the campaign/account management rail, tightens canvas/tool sizing for narrow screens, and keeps controls reachable without sticky topbar/console overlays. The mobile Playwright smoke now asserts the campaign heading renders before the session rail on both phone and tablet viewports while retaining reload coverage for the demo player.
- 2026-05-13 chat-history follow-up: the browser now has a Chat tab with full campaign chat history, text search, type filtering, visibility filtering, timestamps, scene/roll metadata, and message counts, while the bottom console remains a compact recent-message preview. The Playwright smoke now checks the chat-history controls.
- 2026-05-13 roll-history follow-up: the API now exposes `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/rolls` using the existing dice/chat visibility rules, the browser snapshot loads visible roll records, and the Chat tab now includes a Roll History section with totals, formulas, labels, visibility, timestamps, and per-term result breakdowns.
- 2026-05-13 saved-dice-formulas follow-up: the bottom dice console now has browser-persisted saved formulas with a picker and one-click save action, and the Playwright smoke checks both controls.
- 2026-05-13 campaign dice-macros follow-up: campaign-shared dice macros are now first-class state exported with campaign archives and managed by `GET|POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/dice-macros` plus `PATCH|DELETE /api/v1/dice-macros/{macroId}` behind explicit campaign permissions. The browser formula picker includes shared campaign macros, and the GM save action publishes the current formula as a campaign macro instead of only writing local browser state.
- 2026-05-13 chat-whisper-moderation follow-up: chat message creation now validates whisper recipients against campaign membership, treats `type: "whisper"` as whisper visibility, and requires at least one recipient. `DELETE /api/v1/chat/messages/{messageId}` is available only behind `chat.moderate`, broadcasts `chat.message.deleted`, and the browser Chat tab shows moderator-only delete controls plus readable sender/recipient labels. The footer chat composer now includes a whisper recipient picker and the Playwright smoke checks that control.
- 2026-05-13 campaign asset-quota UX follow-up: the browser snapshot now loads `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/assets/storage`, and the Content tab shows campaign asset quota, remaining bytes, active bytes, lifecycle/provider counts, largest asset rows, and inline upload failure status. The Playwright smoke checks the campaign quota posture in the Asset Library.
- 2026-05-13 chat export/audit follow-up: `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/chat/export?format=json|ndjson` exports only chat messages visible to the caller and appends redacted `chat.export` audit rows with type/visibility counts. Moderator message deletion now appends redacted `chat.message.delete` audit rows without storing message bodies, and the browser Chat tab exposes an export action checked by Playwright.
- 2026-05-13 token state/targeting follow-up: tokens now carry notes, conditions, auras, and targeted-by user ids. Token create/update validates those fields, `POST /api/v1/tokens/{tokenId}/target` lets users target visible tokens without broad token-update permission, and the browser actor/token inspector exposes condition, aura, note, and target controls with Playwright smoke coverage.
- 2026-05-13 combat audit-history follow-up: combat start, update/round advance, and end now append redacted combat audit rows. `GET /api/v1/combats/{combatId}/audit` exposes a combat-scoped audit history to campaign readers, and the browser Combat tab renders recent combat audit entries with Playwright coverage.
- 2026-05-13 scene annotation tools follow-up: `Scene` now carries permissioned `ping`, `ruler`, `template`, and `drawing` annotations. `POST /api/v1/scenes/{sceneId}/annotations` lets readers create pings/rulers and scene editors create templates/drawings; `DELETE /api/v1/scenes/{sceneId}/annotations/{annotationId}` removes annotations behind `scene.update`. The browser tabletop toolbar exposes pointer-driven ping, ruler, area-template, drawing, and delete-latest controls, and the seeded Playwright tabletop smoke verifies ping/ruler/template creation plus deletion.
- 2026-05-13 campaign archive-restore follow-up: campaigns now have non-destructive archive/restore metadata (`archivedAt`, `archivedByUserId`, `restoredAt`, `restoredByUserId`). `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/archive` and `/restore` require `campaign.update`, preserve scenes/tokens/assets, broadcast campaign updates, and write redacted lifecycle audit rows. The browser Campaign Settings panel shows active/archived status and exposes Archive/Restore controls verified by the seeded Playwright tabletop smoke.
- 2026-05-13 scene background picker follow-up: the browser Scene Manager now renders a background preview, exposes direct background asset selectors on scene create/edit, and shows image thumbnails in scene tabs. The seeded Playwright tabletop smoke verifies assigning, clearing, and reassigning an uploaded SVG scene background through the Scene Manager.
- 2026-05-13 AI proposal diff follow-up: browser proposal cards now include a structured Review Diff region listing each proposed entity/action and key field values before approval. The seeded Playwright AI smoke verifies the encounter proposal diff is visible before approve/apply.
- 2026-05-13 auth connection-test follow-up: server admins can now run `POST /api/v1/admin/auth/test-connection` for `oidc` or `scim`. OIDC checks required config and discovery metadata, SCIM checks bearer endpoint readiness, both return only redacted pass/block/fail checks, and the bootstrap Playwright admin smoke exercises the Auth Setup test buttons.
- 2026-05-13 content-import browser follow-up: the Content tab now renders a validation report for each content import with source/provenance metadata, license usage, selected/applied counts, multi-entity manual batch staging, per-entity selection checkboxes, warnings, applied-record references, and rollback timestamps. The seeded Playwright tabletop smoke stages a journal plus handout, previews the browser content import, verifies provenance/license review, proves empty selection blocks apply, applies only the selected journal, and rolls it back with history visible.
- 2026-05-13 actor compendium browser follow-up: the selected-actor panel now fetches the actor's rules-system compendium, shows searchable item/spell/condition entries with summaries and key metadata, and imports the chosen entry through the existing permissioned system compendium route. The seeded Playwright tabletop smoke searches for `Healing Word`, adds it to Valen Ash, and verifies the actor sheet surfaces it under Spells.
- 2026-05-13 admin production-readiness checklist follow-up: the Server Admin panel now starts with a production readiness checklist that rolls auth, asset storage, asset integrity, rendering, rules systems, AI operations, plugin marketplace, and audit export telemetry into ready/action/missing states with concise remediation details. The clean-bootstrap Playwright admin smoke verifies the checklist region and core readiness rows before the deeper admin sections.
- 2026-05-13 API contract policy follow-up: `@open-tabletop/api-contracts` now exports a shared v1 `apiContractPolicy`, and `GET /api/v1/openapi.json` serves an OpenAPI 3.1 seed with bearer auth, standard `ErrorResponse`/`PaginationMeta` components, path parameters, idempotency-key headers for mutations, list pagination/filter/sort parameters, rate-limit response headers, selected import/export/content-import/runtime routes, and top-level v1 compatibility/auth/error/idempotency/pagination/rate-limit policy extensions. The API regression suite now verifies the served policy document.
- 2026-05-13 seeded E2E determinism follow-up: the seeded Playwright config now runs with one worker because the current browser specs intentionally share one seeded API store while the tabletop smoke mutates campaign/scene state. This removes cross-spec interference from the `pnpm e2e` gate until the suite is split into per-test isolated stores.
- 2026-05-13 SQLite storage operations follow-up: `SqliteStateStore` now exposes server-admin storage posture for the JSON-record persistence model, including applied migrations, required indexes, `PRAGMA integrity_check`, collection record counts, latest backup summary, and action reasons. New `GET /api/v1/admin/storage/operations`, `POST /api/v1/admin/storage/backup`, and `POST /api/v1/admin/storage/restore-drill` routes let admins create timestamped SQLite backups and verify a copied backup opens, migrates, passes integrity checks, and loads campaign records. API coverage verifies admin-only access, backup creation, successful drill, and missing-backup failure behavior.
- 2026-05-13 SQLite storage admin UI follow-up: the Server Admin panel now pulls `GET /api/v1/admin/storage/operations` into the production readiness checklist, renders SQLite database, migration, index, integrity, record, backup, and restore-drill posture in a dedicated SQLite Storage section, and exposes Create Backup plus Run Restore Drill controls. The clean-bootstrap Playwright admin smoke creates a backup and verifies the restore drill passes from the browser.
- 2026-05-13 SQLite storage worker CLI follow-up: the worker JSON-job CLI now supports server-admin `storage.backup` and `storage.restoreDrill` jobs, routing them to `/api/v1/admin/storage/backup` and `/api/v1/admin/storage/restore-drill` with bearer-session auth. Worker coverage verifies the exact API paths, payloads, and outputs, and the backup/restore runbook documents CLI examples for pre-upgrade backups and drills.
- 2026-05-13 SQLite scheduled-backup follow-up: the API now has optional in-process SQLite backup scheduling via `OTTE_SQLITE_BACKUP_RUN_ON_START`, `OTTE_SQLITE_BACKUP_INTERVAL_SECONDS`, and `OTTE_SQLITE_BACKUP_REASON`. Scheduled runs create the same timestamped SQLite backups as the admin route, append redacted `system.storage.backupScheduled` audit rows, stop their interval on app close, and surface enabled/running/interval/latest-run status through `/api/v1/admin/storage/operations` and the Admin UI. API coverage verifies startup scheduling creates a real backup and reports the scheduled run.
- 2026-05-13 backup rollback runbook follow-up: `docs/deployment/backup-restore.md` now includes an explicit rollback procedure for failed upgrades, migrations, imports, or maintenance, including evidence preservation, SQLite/asset/plugin/env restore order, previous-code restart, storage integrity and restore-drill validation, campaign export, GM browser smoke, and incident-note fields. The upgrade guide now points rollback operators to that procedure.
- 2026-05-13 SQLite support-boundary follow-up: `docs/deployment/self-hosting.md` and `docs/api/rest.md` now define the v1 SQLite JSON-record support boundary: a single API writer, internal `engine_records`/`schema_migrations` tables, REST/realtime/SDK/archive APIs as the public integration surface, SQLite backups as same-deployment recovery artifacts, forward-only migrations with rollback by restore, file/memory stores as development-only, and multi-node SQLite access outside the v1 boundary.
- 2026-05-13 admin audit export UI follow-up: the Server Admin Audit section now exposes action, target type, actor type, campaign, and limit filters, plus an Export Redacted JSON action that calls `/api/v1/admin/audit-logs` and downloads the redacted audit bundle. The clean-bootstrap Playwright admin smoke fills an action filter, runs the export, and verifies the export status.
- 2026-05-13 accessibility smoke follow-up: `tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts` now checks visible main-tabletop buttons, inputs, selects, textareas, and links for accessible names, then verifies keyboard focus and Enter activation for Add Token, Roll Dice, and Chat navigation controls.
- 2026-05-13 accessibility polish follow-up: `apps/web/src/styles.css` now applies the focus-visible treatment to links, tab-like controls, icon/tool buttons, and tabindex-managed controls, disables animated transitions under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, and adds text/symbol prefixes to status pills so completed/failed/running states do not depend on color alone. The accessibility smoke emulates reduced motion and asserts the focused Add Token control receives a visible outline and suppressed transition duration.
- 2026-05-13 security smoke follow-up: root `pnpm security:smoke` now runs `apps/api/src/security-smoke.test.ts`, covering server-admin auth boundaries, unsafe upload blocking before asset persistence, signed asset URL validation without signing-secret disclosure, local asset path containment, and trusted-only plugin install/command enforcement.
- 2026-05-13 performance smoke follow-up: root `pnpm perf:smoke` now runs `apps/api/src/performance-smoke.test.ts`, seeding 250 extra scene tokens, 400 chat rows, 80 journal rows, and two extra campaign members, then checking large scene-token reads, chat export, D&D compendium lookup, campaign archive export, and four-client realtime fanout against explicit smoke budgets.
- 2026-05-13 migration smoke follow-up: root `pnpm migration:smoke` now runs `apps/api/src/migration-smoke.test.ts` against a clean SQLite store, imports the v0.1 public-alpha and v0.2 beta archive fixtures, re-exports both as current `0.2.0` archives, verifies SQLite migration/index/integrity posture, creates a backup, runs a successful restore drill, and rejects an unsupported archive version.
- 2026-05-13 release smoke follow-up: root `pnpm release:smoke` now bundles `pnpm check`, seeded and clean-bootstrap Playwright E2E, security smoke, migration smoke, and performance smoke into one executable release-hardening gate.
- 2026-05-13 campaign setup wizard follow-up: the browser Campaign Setup form now collects campaign basics, rules system, visibility, custom initial scene name/folder/dimensions/grid, a starter invite with role-based permission preset, and optional public onboarding handout copy. `createCampaignFromSetup` now creates the campaign, first scene, optional onboarding journal, and optional invite in one flow, and the seeded Playwright suite verifies a GM can complete that wizard and see the new campaign, scene, invite token, and onboarding journal.
- 2026-05-13 combat depth follow-up: `Combatant` now supports optional `readiness`, `conditions`, `deathSaveSuccesses`, `deathSaveFailures`, `resourceLabel`, and `resourceUsed` fields. The API adds `PATCH /api/v1/combats/{combatId}/combatants/{combatantId}` so combatant edits merge server-side instead of replacing stale whole-combat snapshots, combat audit summaries include the new state, and the browser Combat Tracker exposes ready/delayed state, combat conditions, death saves, and resource-used tracking. The seeded Playwright combat lifecycle test now verifies those controls before ending combat.
- 2026-05-13 system action targeting follow-up: the selected-actor panel now lets a user choose an action target actor, decide whether to apply damage/healing to that target, and decide whether to consume spell slots, item charges, or class resources when using the actor's first imported system action. The browser posts those options through the existing permissioned actor-action route, reports applied effects after refresh, and the seeded Playwright tabletop smoke imports Healing Word, targets Valen Ash, applies the effect without resource consumption, and verifies the applied result is visible.
- 2026-05-13 combat actor-sheet sync follow-up: combatant edits can now request `syncActorSheet` on `PATCH /api/v1/combats/{combatId}/combatants/{combatantId}`. When requested, the API requires actor update permission, writes combat conditions, death saves, readiness/defeated state, and resource-used state onto the linked actor sheet, broadcasts `actor.updated`, and still permits combat-only edits when sync is not requested. The browser combat tracker requests sync for actor-linked state edits and the Actor panel now surfaces synced Combat State rows; focused API and Playwright coverage verify both the permission boundary and the browser write-back path.
- 2026-05-13 actor sheet controls follow-up: the selected-actor panel now has direct condition and resource-current edit controls alongside HP editing, using the existing actor update route. Generic numeric resources such as Valen Ash's `focus` pool now render in the Resources row, and the combat lifecycle Playwright test verifies editing actor conditions and focus from the browser after combat sync.
- 2026-05-13 asset batch lifecycle follow-up: the Asset Library now supports selecting individual assets or all visible filtered assets, then batch archiving, restoring, or deleting them through the existing asset lifecycle route. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke uploads `e2e-map.svg`, edits its metadata, batch archives it, verifies the archived status, batch restores it, and then continues with scene background assignment.

- 2026-05-13 mobile player-reload follow-up: the browser now waits for an authenticated campaign snapshot before rendering the tabletop shell, clears stale campaign and scene ids when the accessible snapshot changes, and the phone/tablet Playwright smoke switches to Demo Player, reloads, and verifies it returns to The Ember Vault instead of an empty campaign header.

- 2026-05-13 plugin-system marketplace review follow-up: the GM Runtime SDK panel now surfaces plugin trust policy/status, marketplace review status, signature/checksum, sandbox diagnostics, requested/granted/missing permission review, latest/update/rollback version posture, and sends the reviewed permission set during install. System cards now show bundled manifest validation, compendium coverage, migration posture, and activation impact, and the SDK Playwright smoke verifies those review signals before installing the example plugin and running the system workflow.

- 2026-05-13 AI proposal review queue follow-up: the GM AI panel now has a Review Queue section with status and search filters, pending/approved/applied/rejected/stale review counts, failed thread/tool recovery signals, and explicit apply-permission copy explaining that AI/plugin work remains reviewable until a permitted user applies it. The AI Playwright smoke now verifies applied and rejected proposal history through the filters after approve/apply and reject actions.

- 2026-05-13 AI proposal timeline follow-up: each GM AI proposal card now includes a Review Timeline derived from proposal metadata, showing creation source, linked AI/plugin source ids, approval facts, current status, stale review age, and whether applied/rejected proposals mutated campaign state. The seeded AI Playwright smoke verifies pending, applied, and rejected timeline states remain visible inside the proposal history queue.

- 2026-05-13 AI proposal existing-record diff follow-up: proposal review diffs now compare update/delete changes against the loaded campaign snapshot for campaigns, scenes, tokens, actors, items, journals, chat, encounters, and combats. The browser shows Current versus Proposed values for changed fields when the target record is present, and the seeded AI Playwright smoke seeds a scene-update proposal to verify the existing-record comparison in the GM review queue.

- 2026-05-13 AI proposal lifecycle history follow-up: proposals now persist optional lifecycle history entries for create, approve, reject, apply, and server-admin stale rejection cleanup. Entries include exact timestamps, previous/final status, actor type/user id, and the redacted audit action name, and the GM Review Timeline prefers those stored events while retaining derived fallback rendering for older proposals.

- 2026-05-13 chat moderation review follow-up: the Chat tab now includes a moderator-only review panel with counts for reviewable non-public/automation messages, whispers, GM-only messages, and AI/plugin automation, plus quick filters for whispers, GM-only, AI, and all chat. The seeded tabletop browser smoke verifies the moderation review region and whisper filter are visible to the demo GM.

- 2026-05-13 asset restore recovery follow-up: the Content tab now has direct lifecycle filtering plus an Asset Restore Recovery panel that counts archived/deleted recoverable assets, lists recent recovery candidates, can select all recoverable assets, and restores the selected set through the existing lifecycle route. The seeded tabletop browser smoke verifies the lifecycle filter, recovery panel, archived filter, and batch restore flow on an uploaded map asset.

- 2026-05-13 orchestrator recipe follow-up: `docs/deployment/self-hosting.md` now goes beyond local Docker Compose with production orchestrator guidance for single-writer SQLite, platform secrets, health/readiness probes, systemd API plus templated worker services, Kubernetes-style one-replica API plus multi-replica worker deployments, and hosted-preview limitations when no writable SQLite volume is available.

- 2026-05-13 actor sheet loadout follow-up: the selected Actor panel now has sheet view tabs for stats, loadout, actions, and compendium. The loadout view exposes spell/talent/ritual preparation, gear equipped state, and quantity editing through a new permissioned PATCH /api/v1/items/{itemId} route that accepts actor.update or owned actor.updateOwned. API coverage verifies GM item preparation updates and observer denial, and the tabletop Playwright smoke toggles Healing Word preparation from the browser before using the imported system action.

- 2026-05-13 actor/asset placement follow-up: the tabletop now accepts token placement payloads on the scene canvas. The selected-actor panel exposes draggable actor placement chips, the Asset Library exposes image-asset token placement controls with the same drag payload plus a click fallback, and created tokens are selected after placement. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke drags Valen Ash from the actor tray onto the scene and places uploaded `e2e-map.svg` from the asset list as a token.

- 2026-05-13 plugin marketplace upgrade/rollback follow-up: the GM Runtime SDK panel now sends explicit plugin package versions when installing older versions, upgrading to the latest version, or rolling back to a prior version. A bundled `versioned-browser-plugin` ships 1.0.0 and 2.0.0 packages with distinct `/versioned` command output, and the seeded SDK Playwright smoke installs 1.0.0, verifies the v1 command, upgrades to 2.0.0, verifies the v2 command, and rolls back to 1.0.0.

- 2026-05-13 combat outcome automation follow-up: combatant PATCH now applies a generic rules automation pass before persistence. Three death-save successes mark a synced actor/combatant stable, three failures mark the combatant defeated/dead, timed condition labels such as `restrained:2` decrement on round advancement, and the resource-used toggle depletes/restores the linked actor resource once when actor-sheet sync is permitted. Focused API coverage verifies permissioned actor sync, stable/dead outcomes, timed condition countdown, and resource depletion; the seeded combat Playwright smoke verifies the stable/depleted labels in the browser.

- 2026-05-13 token ownership editor follow-up: tokens now carry optional `ownerUserIds` as an explicit ownership override alongside actor-derived ownership. Token create/update validates owners against campaign members, `token.move` still requires ownership for non-GM users, and the selected-token inspector exposes campaign-member owner checkboxes. Focused API coverage verifies an unowned token blocks player movement until the GM assigns the player as owner, and the seeded GM Playwright smoke verifies assigning Demo Player from the browser.

- 2026-05-13 token permission preset follow-up: the selected-token inspector now adds GM-facing presets on top of the existing ownership/lock/hidden fields: GM locked, party controlled, target only, and hidden hold. The browser labels the current preset/custom state, keeps updates behind `token.update`, and the seeded GM Playwright smoke verifies switching between party-controlled and GM-locked presets.

- 2026-05-13 token-context action targeting follow-up: the selected-actor panel now derives action-target shortcut buttons from the selected token and tokens targeted by the current user, keeping the existing `targetActorId` actor-action API while giving GMs a canvas-linked targeting path. The focused GM Playwright smoke verifies the selected-token target shortcut and marked-target label before switching token permission presets.
- 2026-05-13 multi-token canvas targeting follow-up: the selected Actor panel now includes a Canvas Targets manager that reuses `POST /api/v1/tokens/{tokenId}/target` to mark individual scene tokens, target all visible scene tokens, target hostile tokens, and clear the current user's scene targets. The focused GM Playwright smoke places a second token, targets both visible tokens, verifies the `2 / 2` target count, clears both targets, and then returns to the selected-token permission preset path.

- 2026-05-13 campaign permission-template follow-up: campaign creation now accepts a `permissionTemplate` value and materializes it as role-scoped `PermissionGrant` records, with core permission checks and member permission snapshots honoring active role grants. The setup wizard exposes Standard table, Player authoring, AI-assisted players, and Assistant GM ops templates with inline setup copy, and focused API/browser coverage verifies the player-authoring path.
- 2026-05-13 campaign setup-impact preview follow-up: the Campaign Setup wizard now renders a live impact preview for access/invite posture, initial scene size/grid/folder, selected permission template, and onboarding handout creation before submission. The setup wizard Playwright test verifies the preview updates after selecting invite-only player access, player-authoring permissions, custom scene dimensions, and onboarding copy.

- 2026-05-13 destructive SQLite restore follow-up: `SqliteStateStore` can now restore the live JSON-record database from a named backup after first running the existing restore-drill validation. The server-admin API exposes `POST /api/v1/admin/storage/restore` with exact filename confirmation, redacted audit logging, OpenAPI schema coverage, and focused API coverage proving post-backup state is removed after restore. The Admin SQLite Storage panel now surfaces the destructive restore confirmation field and Restore Backup action, and the clean-bootstrap Playwright smoke exercises the guarded UI path.

- 2026-05-13 idempotency replay follow-up: mutating API requests with `Idempotency-Key` now persist successful JSON responses in `EngineState.idempotencyRecords`, scoped by method, path, caller, and stable request body hash. Matching retries replay the original response with `Idempotency-Replayed: true`; reusing the key for a different request returns 409. API contract docs now describe the runtime guarantee, and focused plus full API regression coverage verifies replay without duplicate scene creation.

- 2026-05-13 OpenAPI live-route conformance follow-up: the OpenAPI seed now documents every live REST path/method registered in `apps/api/src/app.ts`, including newer admin recovery/operations, scene wall/light/fog updates, actor/item updates, proposal actions, AI memory/evaluation/session endpoints, system origin/monster/purchase endpoints, and the dogfood report bundle. The OpenAPI API regression test now compares the served spec against live route registrations and fails on missing REST path/method coverage while excluding the websocket-only realtime route.

- 2026-05-13 API rate-limit enforcement follow-up: the API now has a fixed-window per-route caller limiter that is enabled by default in production or explicitly with `OTTE_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true`. `OTTE_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_SECONDS` and `OTTE_RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS` tune the window and ceiling, throttled requests return `429 rate_limited` with `Retry-After`, `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset`, and focused API coverage verifies both enforcement and headers.

- 2026-05-13 API client conformance follow-up: `@open-tabletop/api-client` now wraps the remaining public REST route surface for auth/account, campaign members, scene vision/rendering/fog/walls/lights, asset storage/upload/delivery, item updates, combatant patching, encounters/proposals, AI memory/evaluation/session/encounter routes, plugin storage/commands/registry sync, system character/monster/compendium/action routes, content-import lifecycle, and dogfood report bundles. A new client regression test drives real client methods through a fake fetch and verifies the emitted method/path calls cover every non-admin, non-SCIM, non-websocket route in the OpenAPI seed, with explicit exclusions for binary asset blobs and the OIDC browser callback.
- 2026-05-13 OpenAPI route-schema follow-up: `@open-tabletop/api-contracts` now defines concrete JSON request/response components for the first high-traffic public route slice: health, owner bootstrap, login/session, campaign CRUD/member listing, campaign scene listing/creation, scene CRUD, token listing/creation/update/delete, chat list/create/delete, dice roll creation, and campaign roll listing. The served OpenAPI API regression test now asserts those routes expose typed JSON bodies instead of generic `200 OK` placeholders.
- 2026-05-13 OpenAPI gameplay-schema follow-up: the route-specific OpenAPI schema coverage now extends to asset metadata/upload/lifecycle/delivery routes, actor create/update/listing, item create/update/listing, journal create/update/listing, combat start/update/combatant/audit/end routes, and proposal create/approve/reject/apply routes. The served OpenAPI regression test now checks representative request and response schemas from this second public gameplay slice.
- 2026-05-13 server-admin job ledger follow-up: `EngineState` now persists `jobs` records, including SQLite JSON-record persistence, and the API exposes server-admin-only `GET|POST /api/v1/admin/jobs`, `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/lease`, `GET /api/v1/admin/jobs/operations`, `GET /api/v1/admin/jobs/metrics`, `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/alerts`, `GET|PATCH /api/v1/admin/jobs/{jobId}`, `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/{jobId}/heartbeat`, `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/{jobId}/retry`, and `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs/{jobId}/cancel`. Job responses redact raw archives, AI source text/transcripts, and sensitive log details while preserving operational progress, attempts, leases, heartbeats, output/error, cancellation, and bounded logs. The operations endpoint summarizes full-ledger queue age, stale queued work, expired leases, stale heartbeats, retry exhaustion, worker posture, and remediation rows; the metrics endpoint returns server-admin-gated Prometheus text gauges for the same queue, lease, heartbeat, retry, and remediation posture; the alerts endpoint dry-runs or delivers redacted job posture to `OTTE_JOB_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL`. The Server Admin panel now loads recent jobs and job operations into the production-readiness checklist and shows a Job Ledger section with posture metrics plus retry/cancel and alert controls, and the clean-bootstrap Playwright smoke verifies the section is visible. The worker CLI can run one leased job via `OTTE_WORKER_LEASE_ONCE=true` or continuously poll leases via `OTTE_WORKER_LEASE_POLL=true`, heartbeat each job while dispatching, abort in-flight API requests when heartbeat rejection indicates cancellation, dispatch through the existing API bridge, and patch success/failure. Docker Compose now includes an opt-in `worker` profile built from `infra/docker/worker.Dockerfile`; it waits for the API healthcheck, runs the polling worker with `restart: unless-stopped`, and documents the required `OTTE_WORKER_SESSION_TOKEN`. Focused API coverage verifies admin-only access, create/list/read/lease/heartbeat/update/retry/cancel flows, retry accounting, operations posture, metrics export, alert dry-run/missing-webhook/delivery, and redaction; worker coverage verifies lease, heartbeat, dispatch, success patch, idle, bounded polling, and in-flight cancellation aborts; the served OpenAPI contract now has concrete schemas for the job routes, operations posture, metrics export, and alert delivery.
- 2026-05-13 job alert browser workflow follow-up: the Server Admin Job Ledger now includes an explicit Alert delivery posture row explaining whether dry-run or send-alert work is needed and which webhook environment variable enables delivery. The clean-bootstrap Playwright admin smoke now runs the Dry Run Alert action from the browser and verifies the dry-run result, complementing the existing API coverage for missing-webhook and delivered webhook alert cases.
- 2026-05-13 storage job scheduling UI follow-up: the Admin SQLite Storage panel now has Queue Backup Job and Queue Drill Job controls that create persisted `storage.backup` and `storage.restoreDrill` records through `POST /api/v1/admin/jobs`. The clean-bootstrap Playwright admin smoke queues a backup job from the storage panel and verifies it appears in the Job Ledger.
- 2026-05-13 SCIM setup mapping browser follow-up: the clean-bootstrap admin smoke now exercises the Organization Access SCIM mapping form by creating a pending group-display-name mapping from `Bootstrap Players` to the starter campaign's player role. This verifies the browser setup flow beyond static IdP guidance and redacted OIDC/SCIM connection-test buttons.
- 2026-05-13 production-readiness playbook follow-up: the Server Admin production readiness panel now renders remediation playbooks for each readiness check, pairing the current ready/action/missing status with operator actions and proof targets for auth, SQLite, jobs, assets, rendering, rules, AI, plugins, and audit export. The clean-bootstrap admin smoke verifies the playbook section and key auth/storage instructions are visible before exercising the deeper admin controls.

- 2026-05-13 accessibility smoke follow-up: the main tabletop accessibility Playwright smoke now verifies the named workspace main landmark and Campaigns navigation landmark, rejects duplicate DOM ids, checks computed 4.5:1 contrast for visible interactive/status text samples, and keeps the previous accessible-name, reduced-motion, focus-visible, and keyboard activation coverage for Add Token, Roll Dice, and Chat. The shell main and campaign navigation now have explicit accessible names for screen-reader orientation.

- 2026-05-13 campaign asset-delivery diagnostics follow-up: `GET /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/assets/storage` now includes a redacted delivery diagnostics block with signed/CDN mode, signing and purge readiness, TTL posture, warning/action reasons, affected environment variable names only, and campaign-scoped deliverable/undeliverable managed asset counts. The Content tab surfaces those CDN/signed-delivery diagnostics next to quota and storage pressure without exposing CDN URLs, webhook URLs, signing secrets, tokens, signatures, or object bytes.

- 2026-05-13 multi-worker orchestration follow-up: the worker now falls back to a sanitized host/container name when `OTTE_WORKER_ID` is blank, so scaled Docker Compose worker replicas lease with distinct ids by default. The Compose worker profile no longer forces a shared `worker-compose-1` id, and the self-hosting guide documents `docker compose --scale worker=N`, single-API-writer boundaries, lease/poll interval tuning, one-off maintenance bounds, and job operations signals to watch before adding capacity.

- 2026-05-13 asset upload recovery follow-up: the Asset Library now preserves the last failed upload attempt, including file, folder, tag, and upload-as-background intent, and shows retry plus dismiss controls beside the failure status. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke forces an unsafe SVG upload failure, retries the captured attempt, dismisses the recovery row, and then completes a clean upload path.
- 2026-05-13 canvas asset-picker follow-up: the tabletop workspace now includes a Canvas Asset Picker with image-asset selection plus place-token and set-background actions, reusing existing token/background permission checks without requiring GMs to navigate the full asset library list. The seeded asset Playwright flow selects the uploaded `e2e-map.svg` from the canvas picker, places it as a token, verifies the placement status, and cleans up the token.
- 2026-05-14 canvas asset-picker preview follow-up: the Canvas Asset Picker now shows the selected image asset's thumbnail, folder, size, and tags before place-token or set-background actions. The seeded asset Playwright flow verifies the uploaded `e2e-map.svg` preview image and revised folder metadata before placing it from the canvas picker.
- 2026-05-14 canvas asset-folder filter follow-up: the Canvas Asset Picker now has an image-asset folder filter using the same normalized descendant-folder matching as the Asset Library. The seeded asset Playwright flow filters the canvas picker to `maps/revised` before selecting and placing the uploaded `e2e-map.svg`.
- 2026-05-14 canvas asset-thumbnail grid follow-up: the Canvas Asset Picker now includes a filtered thumbnail grid in the canvas toolbar, so GMs can browse image assets visually without opening the full Asset Library. The seeded asset Playwright flow selects the uploaded `e2e-map.svg` from the thumbnail grid, verifies the dropdown and preview sync, and places it on the scene.
- 2026-05-14 canvas asset bulk-placement follow-up: the Canvas Asset Picker now includes a bounded placement count and creates a small row of selected image-asset tokens with one refresh/status update. The seeded asset Playwright flow sets the placement count to two, places two `e2e-map.svg` tokens from the canvas picker, verifies the bulk-placement status, and deletes both placed tokens.
- 2026-05-14 canvas asset drag-preview follow-up: filtered Canvas Asset Picker thumbnail tiles are now draggable token-placement sources with the same permissioned token-drop payload as the Asset Library plus a native drag preview showing the image and asset name. The seeded asset Playwright flow drags the uploaded `e2e-map.svg` thumbnail from the canvas picker onto the scene, verifies placement, and deletes the placed token.
- 2026-05-14 canvas asset search follow-up: the Canvas Asset Picker now searches the currently folder-filtered image assets by name, folder, and tags before updating the dropdown and thumbnail grid. The seeded asset Playwright flow filters to `maps/revised`, searches `e2e-map`, and then selects the uploaded map from the narrowed thumbnail grid before preview, bulk placement, and drag placement checks.
- 2026-05-13 actor loose-item assignment follow-up: `PATCH /api/v1/items/{itemId}` now accepts same-campaign `actorId` reassignment with explicit actor-update or owned-actor permission checks and broadcasts actor updates for the previous/new sheet. The selected actor Loadout tab now lists unassigned campaign items and assigns the selected loose item onto the actor; the seeded browser content-import flow imports an item, assigns it to Valen Ash from the Loadout tab, verifies it appears on the sheet, then rolls the import back.
- 2026-05-14 actor loose-item drag assignment follow-up: the selected actor Loadout panel now exposes unassigned campaign items as draggable loose-item chips, accepts item drops on the loadout sheet, and reuses the same permissioned `PATCH /api/v1/items/{itemId}` reassignment path as the selector. The seeded browser content-import flow now assigns `E2E Import Ledger` by dragging it onto Valen Ash's Loadout sheet before verifying it appears and rolling the import back.
- 2026-05-14 loadout quantity-stepper follow-up: loadout items with quantities now have Spend one and Add one controls beside the direct quantity field, all behind the existing actor-update/item PATCH path. The seeded browser content-import flow assigns `E2E Import Ledger`, increments it to quantity 2, verifies the loadout label, spends it back to quantity 1, and then rolls the import back.
- 2026-05-14 actor equipment-purchase follow-up: the actor compendium browser now exposes D&D SRD purchasable entries with quantity, unit/total gp cost, remaining-currency feedback, and a Purchase action backed by the existing permissioned system purchase endpoint. The SDK/system Playwright smoke creates a D&D Fighter, links the selected token to that actor, purchases 2 bundles of Arrows, verifies `48 gp remaining`, and confirms `Arrows x2` appears in the Loadout sheet.
- 2026-05-14 advancement-choice browser follow-up: the SDK panel now loads the selected actor's system advancement choices from the existing advancement endpoint, renders a labelled Advancement option selector with summary and next-value preview, and advances with the selected option rather than a hardcoded browser id. The SDK/system Playwright smoke verifies the D&D Fighter exposes `Level 2`, clicks the generic Level Up action, and confirms `Fighter advanced to Level 2`.
- 2026-05-14 initiative-aware targeting follow-up: the Actor panel Canvas Targets manager now reads the active combat turn order and exposes Target current turn plus Target next turn controls alongside visible/hostile/clear target groups. The combat lifecycle Playwright smoke starts combat, opens the Actor panel, verifies the initiative label, targets the current combatant, and checks the scene target count updates through the existing token-target API.
- 2026-05-14 rectangular area targeting follow-up: the Actor panel Canvas Targets manager now includes numeric rectangular target bounds, target-area, and clear-area-targets controls. The selected-token permissions Playwright smoke places a second token, targets both tokens with the rectangle, clears the same area, and verifies the current user's target count updates through the existing token-target API.
- 2026-05-14 target-area preview follow-up: the Actor panel Canvas Targets manager now previews the exact rectangular-area target set before applying or clearing targets, including affected token count and bounded token names. The selected-token permissions Playwright smoke verifies the preview shows both Valen Ash tokens before targeting and clearing the area.
- 2026-05-14 lasso targeting follow-up: the Actor panel Canvas Targets manager can now treat the latest freehand drawing annotation as a lasso, preview affected token count/names, and target or clear that lasso set through the existing token-target API. The selected-token permissions Playwright smoke seeds the same drawing-annotation shape used by the canvas drawing tool around two tokens, verifies the lasso preview, targets both tokens, and clears them again.
- 2026-05-14 combat condition-timing preview follow-up: the Combat tracker now summarizes timed combatant conditions such as `prone:2` as explicit expiry labels, and those labels update after round advancement as the existing rules-automation countdown mutates combat state. The combat lifecycle Playwright smoke verifies `prone expires in 2 rounds`, advances to round 2, and then verifies `prone expires in 1 round`.
- 2026-05-14 chat moderation thread grouping follow-up: the Chat moderation review panel now groups reviewable messages by visibility/type/user into moderation threads with counts, latest timestamps, and a Review Thread quick filter. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke verifies the grouped thread section appears beside the existing moderation metrics, quick filters, export format selector, and delete controls.
- 2026-05-14 chat moderation resolution follow-up: the Chat moderation review panel now tracks session-scoped open, follow-up, and reviewed states for reviewable messages, with metric counts and per-message Mark follow up / Mark reviewed controls. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke marks a reviewable chat message for follow-up, then reviewed, and verifies the moderation resolution metrics remain visible beside grouped review threads.

- 2026-05-14 content-import adapter preset follow-up: the Content Import form now includes adapter preset setup for manual, CSV item list, Markdown handout, and Open SRD JSON imports. The browser shows adapter id/source/license/usage metadata, source URL, source-specific config, and generated entity count before preview, then submits that adapter provenance into the existing content-import preview route. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke verifies a CSV item-list adapter generates `E2E Adapter Torch` and `E2E Adapter Rope`, shows `Source: Adapter` plus `Adapter: csv-item-list-v1`, and deletes the preview before continuing the manual selective-apply flow.

- 2026-05-14 plugin core-compatibility enforcement follow-up: campaign plugin catalog responses now include evaluated core range, server core version, compatibility status, compatibility block text, and per-version compatibility rows for install/upgrade/rollback targets. Plugin install and chat-command execution reject packages whose `compatibleCore` range does not include the current server core, including stale grants left behind after an upgrade. The GM Runtime SDK marketplace adds a core compatibility filter, visible compatible/incompatible status, compatible/blocked version counts, and per-target disabled actions for incompatible versions. Focused API coverage verifies incompatible install/execute rejection without mutating grants or chat plus mixed-version catalogs that allow a compatible older install while blocking an incompatible latest upgrade, and the seeded SDK Playwright smoke verifies the core filter plus visible compatible status before continuing the plugin workflow.

- 2026-05-14 destructive-dialog accessibility follow-up: selected-token deletion now opens a labelled ARIA dialog with `aria-modal`, descriptive copy naming the token and scene, initial focus on Confirm Delete Token, Escape dismissal, and a Tab loop between Confirm and Cancel before the destructive API call can run. The selected-token permission Playwright smoke now confirms through the dialog, and `tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts` adds focused coverage for the dialog's screen-reader name/content, focus loop, Escape close, and Enter confirmation.

- 2026-05-13 IdP setup guidance follow-up: the browser Admin Auth Setup panel now includes IdP-specific setup rows for Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, Google Workspace, and generic OIDC/SCIM beside the existing redacted OIDC/SCIM posture and test-connection actions. `docs/deployment/identity-providers.md` documents provider callback paths, SCIM base paths, environment variables, group mapping, and redacted verification steps without exposing provider URLs, client secrets, bearer tokens, or identity claims.

- 2026-05-13 plugin registry marketplace follow-up: the GM Runtime SDK panel now exposes a Registry Browser count, package source/provenance rows, and a `Sync marketplace registries` action that calls the campaign-scoped configured-registry sync route behind `plugin.install`. Focused API coverage verifies player denial, GM sync success, imported registry package metadata, and the `plugin.registrySync` audit row. The seeded SDK Playwright smoke verifies the marketplace sync control and source/package provenance are visible before installing and running plugins. `docs/plugin-sdk/overview.md` now documents registry catalog/package shape, checksum/signature expectations, permission review, and author publish checks, while `docs/system-sdk/overview.md` adds a system author checklist.

- 2026-05-13 asset quota-management follow-up: the Asset Library now translates campaign asset storage numbers into a Quota Management section with policy state, quota health, remaining headroom, recommended cleanup/configuration action, and the largest cleanup candidate when one exists. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke verifies the quota-management section is visible beside the existing quota and delivery posture.

- 2026-05-13 v0.3 SQLite upgrade smoke follow-up: `SqliteStateStore` now persists `idempotencyRecords` alongside the rest of the JSON-record collections, and `apps/api/src/migration-smoke.test.ts` now includes an explicit v0.3 SQLite JSON-record reopen path. The smoke seeds a persisted v0.3-style store, opens it through the current store, verifies migrations/indexes/integrity, creates and replays an idempotent scene mutation, proves the replay record survives a store restart, creates a backup, runs a restore drill, and exports the upgraded campaign.
- 2026-05-13 v0.3 migration fixture-breadth follow-up: the v0.3 SQLite upgrade smoke now seeds asset metadata/storage/security, role permission grants, plugin storage, content-import evidence, fog presets, and a queued storage job before reopening the store through current persistence. The smoke verifies those collections survive upgrade, appear in restore-drill collection counts, persist after restart where appropriate, and export only campaign-scoped archive-safe records while omitting operational jobs.

- 2026-05-13 plugin marketplace filtering follow-up: the GM Runtime SDK panel now has marketplace search plus source and package-status filters over plugin cards, so GMs can narrow local versus registry packages, installed versus available packages, and upgrade-ready packages before reviewing trust, permissions, signatures, and package provenance. The seeded SDK Playwright smoke filters to the versioned plugin, verifies the example plugin is hidden, clears the filter, and continues the install/upgrade/rollback workflow.

- 2026-05-13 chat export format follow-up: the Chat History panel now exposes a JSON/NDJSON export format selector and sends the chosen format to the existing visible-message chat export route before downloading the matching `.json` or `.ndjson` file. The seeded tabletop Playwright smoke verifies the export format selector is present and can switch to NDJSON beside the existing chat filters and moderator delete control.

- 2026-05-13 advanced-panel accessibility follow-up: `tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts` now includes an advanced-panel smoke that enters Chat, Content, AI, and SDK, verifies labelled controls and named regions for chat export format, asset quota management, AI proposal review queue, and plugin marketplace filters, then focuses and edits/selects those controls from the keyboard-visible path. This broadens accessibility evidence beyond the main tabletop toolbar.
- 2026-05-14 actor-target accessibility follow-up: `tests/e2e/accessibility.spec.ts` now verifies the Actor sheet tablist, Canvas target manager region, Canvas target area group, target-area status preview, latest drawing lasso status preview, and keyboard focus/editing for the target-area X spinbutton before keyboard-activating the Loadout tab and confirming the Actor loadout sheet region.

- 2026-05-13 GM AI recovery follow-up: AI thread records now preserve the original prompt for replay, the GM AI panel exposes Recovery Controls for failed provider threads and retryable failed tool calls, and `POST /api/v1/campaigns/{campaignId}/ai/tool-calls/{toolCallId}/retry` lets a GM replay one campaign-owned retryable failure through the existing proposal-safe tool dispatcher. Focused API coverage verifies player denial, GM retry success, fresh started/result lifecycle records, current permission checks on the original thread user, redacted campaign audit rows, OpenAPI schema coverage, and public client route conformance.

- 2026-05-14 organization member browser follow-up: the browser snapshot now loads `GET /api/v1/organization/members`, and the Server Admin tab shows current organization member roles plus an existing-user email form for adding/updating `admin` or `member` access through `POST /api/v1/organization/members`. The clean-bootstrap admin smoke registers a second user, adds that user to the bootstrapped organization from the browser, and verifies the organization member list updates.

- 2026-05-14 active organization switching follow-up: bearer sessions now carry optional `activeOrganizationId`. `GET /api/v1/organizations` lists accessible organization workspaces with role/member/campaign counts, `PATCH /api/v1/organization/session` switches the active workspace after membership validation, workspace defaults/member routes and campaign creation use that active workspace, and `GET /api/v1/campaigns` scopes results to the selected organization. The browser Account panel exposes the active workspace selector, OpenAPI/API-client coverage includes the routes, and focused API coverage verifies one session switching between two workspaces sees isolated campaign lists.

- 2026-05-14 organization invite administration follow-up: `GET|POST /api/v1/organization/invites` lists invite metadata across campaigns in the active organization for organization owners/admins and lets organization owners/admins create campaign invites for campaigns in that organization without separate campaign-update membership. Organization owners/admins can revoke campaign invites in their organization through the existing invite revoke route. The browser invite panel now creates through the active-organization route, refreshes the active organization invite roster, and shows pending revoke controls. OpenAPI/API-client coverage includes the roster/create route, and focused API coverage verifies scoped invite creation/listing, denied non-admin roster access, denied revoke outside organization admin rights, and successful organization-admin revoke.

- 2026-05-14 plugin trust-policy deployment-matrix follow-up: `apps/api/src/security-smoke.test.ts` now covers production `allow_unsigned` posture, trusted mode without keys, registry-sourced signed packages, `OTTE_PLUGIN_REVIEW_POLICY=require_approved` install blocking, server-admin approval, approved signed install/execution, and local unsigned-package denial under trusted production policy. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/security-smoke.test.ts -t "plugin trust policy posture"`.

- 2026-05-14 organization workspace creation follow-up: `POST /api/v1/organizations` creates an owner workspace for the current bearer session, stores owner membership, selects the new workspace, and returns the refreshed workspace list. The Account panel now exposes a New workspace form beside the active workspace selector. OpenAPI/API-client coverage includes the create route, focused API coverage verifies a user-created workspace starts with an isolated empty campaign list, and the clean-bootstrap browser smoke creates a second workspace, creates a campaign there, verifies selector campaign counts, switches back, and confirms the original campaign is restored. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "workspace defaults"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run -t "serves the OpenAPI contract"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client test -- --run`, and `pnpm exec playwright test --config playwright.bootstrap.config.ts`.

- 2026-05-14 deployment smoke follow-up: root `package.json` now adds `pnpm deployment:smoke` and includes it in `pnpm release:smoke`. `apps/api/src/deployment-smoke.test.ts` verifies release-smoke workflow wiring, Docker Compose API/web/worker Dockerfiles, API SQLite/storage/S3/trust/legacy-auth env posture, API healthcheck, worker service-health dependency and leasing env, persisted storage volumes, self-hosting Docker Compose/worker/secret/preview-only docs, and deployment security checklist coverage for trusted plugin policy, legacy header disablement, and asset signing secrets. Validation passed: `pnpm deployment:smoke` and `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`.

- 2026-05-14 player owned-token browser follow-up: scene token buttons now expose stable accessible names such as `Token E2E Owned ...`, and the two-context invite/realtime Playwright smoke now creates one visible token owned by the accepted player plus one visible unowned token. The accepted player can drag the owned token and the GM API state changes, while the attempted unowned-token drag leaves its position unchanged. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "player can accept an invite"`.

- 2026-05-14 AI memory deletion UI follow-up: `DELETE /api/v1/ai/memory/{factId}` now lets a user with `ai.applyChanges` remove an AI memory fact and writes a redacted `ai.memory.delete` audit row with visibility/source-count/approval metadata but not memory text. The AI panel now renders memory visibility, source ids, and approval user/state before exposing Delete memory, and the seeded AI Playwright smoke approves extracted memory, verifies the metadata, deletes it, and confirms it leaves the list. OpenAPI/API-client/docs now include the delete route. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "approves and applies ai proposals and memory"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run -t "serves the OpenAPI contract"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client test -- --run`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "GM can draft and apply an AI proposal"`.

- 2026-05-14 combat round-three monster workflow follow-up: the browser SDK panel now exposes a D&D SRD Create Monster action backed by the existing monster creation endpoint. The combat Playwright smoke creates a `Goblin Minion`, places it from the actor placement tray onto the scene, starts combat with both Valen Ash and the monster token, edits Valen's HP down then back up to cover damage/healing-style sheet changes, applies combat conditions/death-save/resource state, advances through multi-combatant initiative to round 3, rewinds once, and ends combat. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "GM can run the browser combat tracker lifecycle"`.

- 2026-05-14 scene activation history follow-up: scenes now carry bounded `activationHistory` entries when created active or newly activated, recording activation time, acting user, previous active scene, and deactivated scene ids while ignoring client attempts to patch history directly. The Scene Manager renders activation count and latest activation details, and the seeded tabletop smoke activates a duplicated scene, verifies its previous active scene, deletes it, then reactivates the original scene and verifies the count. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/core build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "keeps scene activation exclusive"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client typecheck`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"`.

- 2026-05-14 multi-scene prep filter follow-up: the tabletop topbar now groups scene prep filters into a folder/search panel with folder counts and a visible/total scene summary, so larger campaigns can narrow scene tabs by folder, name, folder path, or scene id before activating/reordering/editing. The seeded tabletop smoke verifies folder filtering, search hits, empty search state, and clearing the search before continuing scene management. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 scene state comparison follow-up: the browser Scene Manager now compares the selected prep scene against the active player scene, covering dimensions/grid, token count, fog regions, walls, lights, annotations, and background presence. The seeded tabletop smoke verifies the comparison panel on the active scene and again after duplicating into an inactive prep scene, so GMs can inspect drift before activation. The long seeded smoke now has a 60-second timeout because the accumulated v1 coverage was hitting the default 30-second ceiling. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports after one timeout-only rerun.

- 2026-05-14 scene diff detail follow-up: the Scene Manager comparison now adds actionable drift detail rows for active/live state, dimensions and grid, token roster names, fog/wall/light/annotation count drift, and background asset differences. The seeded tabletop smoke verifies the duplicated prep scene shows the drift review section, token roster detail, no token roster drift, and background asset detail before continuing scene selection and bulk move workflows. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 bulk scene-prep action follow-up: the browser scene filter panel now lets GMs enter a bulk folder target and move only the currently visible filtered scene set to that folder through the existing scene update route. The seeded tabletop smoke filters to the duplicated `Vault Entry Reorder` prep scene, moves that one visible scene into `prep/bulk`, verifies the selected scene editor updates, and continues through reorder, activation, and deletion. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 scene multi-select prep follow-up: scene tabs now expose permission-gated selection checkboxes plus scene selection summary, Select visible, Clear selected, and Move selected scenes controls that reuse the existing scene PATCH folder assignment path. The seeded tabletop smoke selects the active and duplicated prep scenes, moves that selected set to `prep/selected`, clears selection, then continues the filtered visible-scene move flow. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 scene bulk-duplicate follow-up: the scene selection toolbar now has a Duplicate selected scenes action that clones every currently selected prep scene through the existing scene creation path, preserving scene dimensions, grid, folder, background, fog, walls, lights, annotations, and metadata while keeping the clones inactive. A focused Playwright smoke creates two uniquely named prep scenes, filters to them, selects both, duplicates the selected set, and verifies both `Copy` scenes appear. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "bulk duplicate selected prep scenes"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 annotation layer/history follow-up: scene annotations now persist layer, group id/label, sort order, and bounded create/delete history entries with actor, action, kind, layer, and group metadata. The canvas controls expose annotation layer and group label inputs plus layer/group summaries and recent annotation history. API coverage verifies player ping grouping/history, GM template layer/group metadata, and delete history; browser coverage verifies group/layer summaries and create/delete history while exercising ping, ruler, and template tools. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/core build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "supports a seeded player session"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client typecheck`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` after one unrelated transient chat-moderation rerun.

- 2026-05-14 template snapping/AOE follow-up: area-template annotations now accept `snapToGrid`, snap points against the scene grid before radius calculation, record `affectedTokenIds`, stamp the campaign rules system, and expose a rules-aware effect hint for the template. The browser annotation panel adds a Snap templates to grid toggle and an Area Template Automation summary showing snapped/free state, affected token count, and rules system. API coverage verifies snapped coordinates, affected-token capture, and D&D effect hints; browser coverage creates a deterministic AOE target token and verifies the snapped template automation summary.
