Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/core build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "supports a seeded player session"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client typecheck`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"`.

- 2026-05-14 template shape/bulk-target follow-up: area-template annotations now carry `templateShape: "circle" | "line" | "cone"`, persist the shape into history/audit summaries, use shape-specific affected-token calculations, and render matching SVG overlays in the tabletop. The annotation panel exposes a Template Shape selector and a Target affected action that applies the existing token-target endpoint to the computed area set. API/browser coverage now exercises a grid-snapped line template, D&D effect hint text, affected-token capture, and bulk target application. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/core build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "supports a seeded player session"`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client typecheck`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 template save/damage adjudication follow-up: area-template annotations now persist save ability, save DC, damage formula, and damage type metadata, carry those fields into annotation history/audit summaries, and include them in rules-aware effect hints. The browser annotation panel exposes save/DC/damage inputs and a Roll damage action for the latest template that posts through the existing dice roll route and campaign roll history. API/browser coverage verifies a line template with Dexterity DC and fire damage metadata, affected tokens, and a deterministic template damage roll. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/core build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-contracts build`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api-client typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "supports a seeded player session"`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 template damage-application follow-up: the annotation panel now has an Apply damage action for the latest area template. It rolls the stored template damage formula, then applies the result to affected linked actors' HP when actor-update permission is available or marks unlinked affected tokens with a damage condition through the existing token-update route. The seeded tabletop smoke verifies applying deterministic fire damage to the `E2E Markup` line-template target and reads the token condition back from the scene-token API. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 template save-resolution follow-up: the annotation panel now has a Resolve saves action for the latest area template. It rolls the stored damage formula once, rolls each affected token's configured ability save with linked-actor ability modifiers when present, applies full damage on failed saves or half damage on successful saves through the existing actor/token update paths, and records the save outcome in unlinked token damage conditions. The seeded tabletop smoke verifies a Dexterity DC 15 save outcome for the `E2E Markup` line-template target and accepts either saved-half or failed-full deterministic condition text. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 template resistance/concentration follow-up: template damage application now adjusts affected-token damage through actor damage-trait data or token/actor condition cues for resistance, immunity, and vulnerability, and annotates concentration check DC when a damaged target is concentrating. The seeded tabletop smoke patches the deterministic AOE target with `Resistant fire` and `Concentrating`, then verifies direct damage is halved with concentration DC notation and save-resolution damage remains halved after save outcome calculation. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 annotation layer visibility follow-up: the annotation panel now exposes per-layer visibility toggles for measurement, effects, drawings, and notes. The canvas filters rendered annotation overlays by each annotation's persisted layer without mutating campaign state, so GMs can bulk hide noisy measurement/effect/drawing layers during prep and restore them immediately. The seeded tabletop smoke hides and restores the Effects layer around a live area template before continuing target and damage automation. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 annotation grouped-delete follow-up: the annotation group summary now includes a permission-gated Delete group action that deletes every annotation sharing the selected group label/id through the existing annotation delete route, preserving per-annotation delete history and avoiding a new bulk mutation endpoint. The seeded tabletop smoke deletes the `E2E Markup` group containing ping, ruler, and template annotations, verifies the grouped cleanup status, and confirms the layer summary returns to empty. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck` and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 annotation group update follow-up: `PATCH /api/v1/scenes/{sceneId}/annotations/{annotationId}` now lets scene editors update annotation label/color/layer/group/order/points/template metadata through the same permission boundary as template/drawing authoring, recomputes template affected-token/effect metadata, records update history/audit summaries, and broadcasts `scene.updated`. The browser annotation group summary now includes Nudge group and Recolor group actions, hides non-control panel surfaces from board hit-testing, and the seeded tabletop smoke verifies group nudge/recolor statuses plus update history before grouped deletion and canvas asset drag/drop. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/web typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run -t "serves the OpenAPI contract"`, and `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "demo GM can reach campaign"` on fresh E2E ports.

- 2026-05-14 audio/video integration documentation follow-up: `docs/deployment/audio-video-integrations.md` now documents the v1 audio/video handoff boundary, supported external provider patterns, campaign-safe link surfaces, identity separation, deployment/media-stack boundaries, provider notes for Discord/Zoom/Google Meet/Jitsi/LiveKit-style setups, operational checks, and explicit non-goals. `docs/deployment/self-hosting.md` and `docs/release/v1.0.md` now link or summarize that RTC handoff posture. Validation passed: `rg -n "Audio and Video Integrations|audio/video|RTC|LiveKit|Jitsi" docs/deployment docs/release docs/prd-v1-gap-closure.md`.

- 2026-05-14 public docs/changelog follow-up: `docs/site/index.md` now acts as the repository-backed public documentation entrypoint, linking GM/player, operator, developer, verification, release, and publication-policy pages. Root `CHANGELOG.md` now records versioned public release history for v1.0 candidate, v0.3 outside dogfood, v0.2 beta, and v0.1 public alpha. `README.md`, `docs/release/v1.0.md`, and this v1 gap tracker now point to those public docs artifacts.

- 2026-05-14 hosted deployment recipes follow-up: `docs/deployment/hosted-deployment-recipes.md` now documents hosted production boundaries and recipes beyond local Docker Compose, including single-VM systemd, container hosts with persistent volumes, Kubernetes-style orchestrators, managed app platforms with volumes, and preview-only static/demo deployments. `docs/deployment/self-hosting.md`, `docs/site/index.md`, `README.md`, `docs/release/v1.0.md`, and this v1 gap tracker now link or summarize that hosted deployment boundary.

- 2026-05-14 AI recovery browser coverage follow-up: the seeded AI Playwright smoke now imports deterministic failed AI thread/tool-call records through the existing campaign archive import path, verifies the browser AI recovery controls show failed-provider replay and retryable `read_compendium` failure details, replays the failed provider prompt, retries the failed tool call, and confirms the retryable row clears after a completed retry. Validation passed: `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "GM can draft and apply an AI proposal"`.

- 2026-05-14 assistive-technology pass-plan follow-up: `docs/verification/accessibility-assistive-tech-pass.md` now defines the manual accessibility evidence required beyond Playwright, including Windows NVDA, Windows Narrator, macOS VoiceOver, iOS/iPadOS VoiceOver, and Android TalkBack environments, covered GM/player/admin workflows, acceptance criteria, stop conditions, and a release evidence template. `docs/site/index.md`, `docs/release/v1.0.md`, and this v1 gap tracker now link or summarize the AT pass plan while preserving the unresolved requirement for completed pass evidence.

- 2026-05-14 docs-site publishing follow-up: root `package.json` now adds `pnpm docs:site` and `pnpm docs:site:check`, backed by `scripts/build-docs-site.mjs`, to render `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `docs/**/*.md` into `dist/docs-site` with a manifest and required-output check. `.github/workflows/docs-site.yml` builds that artifact on docs changes and deploys it to GitHub Pages from `main` when Pages is enabled. `README.md`, `docs/site/index.md`, `docs/release/v1.0.md`, and this v1 gap tracker now describe the local renderer and publication workflow while preserving the need for a live successful Pages run.

- 2026-05-14 toolbar accessibility-label follow-up: the core tabletop toolbar and visible icon-only shell controls now carry explicit ARIA labels while preserving title tooltips. The main accessibility Playwright smoke no longer accepts `title` as the only label source for visible controls and asserts representative toolbar buttons such as Select, Reveal fog, Area template, and Delete latest annotation by accessible name.

- 2026-05-14 annotation drag-handle follow-up: the tabletop canvas now renders permission-gated per-annotation drag handles in select mode for visible annotations. Dragging a handle previews whole-annotation movement locally and submits one annotation PATCH on release. The seeded tabletop smoke moves the `E2E Markup` ruler annotation by its handle, verifies the moved status and update history, then continues through group nudge/recolor/delete cleanup.

- 2026-05-14 combat concentration automation follow-up: combatant rules automation now removes `concentrating`/`concentration` when the combatant becomes stunned, incapacitated, defeated, dead, or stable, then records `concentration lost` so the linked actor sheet sync has explicit evidence. API combat lifecycle coverage and the browser combat tracker smoke now exercise a concentrating Valen becoming stunned/stable and verify the concentration-loss condition sync.

- 2026-05-14 deleted asset lifecycle browser follow-up: the seeded tabletop smoke now deletes the uploaded `e2e-map.svg` asset after archive/restore coverage, verifies the deleted card disables token placement, background assignment, and signed URL creation, confirms the restore recovery panel lists it, then restores it before continuing scene-background assignment.

- 2026-05-14 archive reject-conflicts browser follow-up: the archive Playwright smoke now switches the import wizard to `reject_conflicts`, imports the just-exported valid campaign archive against the same campaign, verifies the operator sees the `import_conflict` failure plus recovery row, then continues through dry-run, selected-collection dependency warnings, skip-conflict import, rollback download, and final upsert recovery.

- 2026-05-14 annotation endpoint-handle follow-up: select mode now renders smaller endpoint handles for two-point ruler/template annotations in addition to the whole-annotation move handle. Dragging a template endpoint submits updated points plus recomputed radius through the existing annotation PATCH route so affected-token/template automation is recalculated server-side. The seeded tabletop smoke now edits the `E2E Markup` template endpoint before group nudge/recolor/delete cleanup.

- 2026-05-14 API behavior compatibility matrix follow-up: the API regression suite now checks representative missing-session behavior across campaign, scene, token, actor, journal, chat/dice, combat, proposal, AI, plugin, system, content-import, and admin route families, and checks read-only observer permission denials across common mutation, AI, plugin, and system routes. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access"`.

- 2026-05-14 admin asset-integrity recovery browser follow-up: the clean-bootstrap admin smoke now drives the Asset Integrity operations panel through a deterministic missing-byte report, verifies the missing asset/remediation, runs Archive broken assets, confirms the quarantine endpoint was invoked, and verifies the panel refreshes to zero actionable integrity findings.

- 2026-05-14 mobile live-play footer follow-up: the phone/tablet Playwright smoke now goes beyond layout and touch Add Token by filling the footer dice formula, tapping Roll dice, sending a chat message from the footer, verifying the live chat preview, and confirming the Chat History panel contains the sent mobile message before continuing the player reload coverage.

- 2026-05-14 organization removal isolation follow-up: the multi-workspace API regression now logs in a user after they were removed from a secondary organization, verifies the organization list no longer includes that workspace, verifies switching the bearer session back to the removed workspace is forbidden, and verifies the removed user's campaign list is empty.

- 2026-05-14 SDK read-only marketplace browser follow-up: a new Demo Player browser smoke opens the SDK panel and verifies marketplace/system registry information remains visible while Sync marketplace registries, plugin Review and install, system Activate, and character-template Create controls are disabled without the required GM permissions.

- 2026-05-14 metadata-backed class-feature condition follow-up: D&D SRD actor rolls can now apply condition effects from top-level roll metadata or failure metadata, so class features such as Monk Stunning Strike can consume Focus and apply `Stunned` to a target through the permissioned actor-roll route. Focused API coverage verifies the quick-roll metadata, resource consumption, returned condition effect, and linked actor condition state. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "supports the D&D 5.5e SRD system as a first-class rules runtime"`.

- 2026-05-14 cross-organization direct-object isolation follow-up: direct-object routes that previously relied on raw campaign permissions now also enforce the bearer session's active organization before campaign archive/restore, fog preset mutation, asset metadata/lifecycle/blob access, scene fog/wall/light/annotation mutation, actor/item updates, journal reads, AI memory reads, and unscoped chat listing. The workspace isolation regression now creates side-workspace scene, asset, actor, item, journal, and chat records, switches the same user back to the original workspace, and verifies cross-active-organization access is denied or scoped out. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "keeps workspace defaults isolated across multiple owner workspaces"`.

- 2026-05-14 expanded API behavior compatibility follow-up: the missing-session compatibility matrix now seeds protected direct-object fixtures and verifies `401` behavior across auth-management, organization/workspace, campaign lifecycle, scene/fog/wall/light/annotation/token, asset metadata/upload/lifecycle/blob, actor/item/journal, chat/dice/macros, encounter/combat, proposal, content-import, AI memory/evaluation/tool/session, plugin storage/command, system character/monster/compendium/action, archive import/export, dogfood report, and server-admin route families. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access"`.

- 2026-05-14 OpenAPI-checked API behavior follow-up: the same missing-session matrix now fetches served `/api/v1/openapi.json`, matches every concrete fixture URL back to its OpenAPI path template, and fails if any user-session-protected REST path/method is not covered by the `401` matrix or explicitly excluded as public/browser-flow/SCIM-bearer provisioning. The matrix now also covers invite revoke plus global plugin/system install and registry-sync routes with explicit campaign context. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access"`.

- 2026-05-14 mutating permission-outcome matrix follow-up: the OpenAPI-checked auth matrix now also exercises the same served protected route fixtures as a read-only observer and verifies `403` for every non-self-service mutating user-session route. Explicit limited-session exceptions are documented in the test for self-service auth, authenticated workspace/campaign creation and workspace switching, read-permitted asset delivery URL, token targeting, encounter planning preview, and archive import. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access"`.

- 2026-05-14 read permission-outcome matrix follow-up: the OpenAPI-checked auth matrix now also exercises protected `GET` fixtures as a read-only observer, expecting `200` for allowed reader routes and `403` for admin-only or higher-permission reads such as invite rosters, fog history, rendering diagnostics, dice macros, AI operations, and plugin storage. The matrix keeps self-service auth reads and fixture-only blob delivery out of the permission-outcome pass and uses the D&D SRD character-origin fixture for a valid system-specific read. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access"`.

- 2026-05-14 organization admin active-workspace scoping follow-up: the multi-workspace API regression now verifies organization member rosters are scoped to the active workspace, do not leak side-workspace members, reject patching a side-workspace organization member id from the demo active workspace, and reject creating an active-organization invite for a campaign that belongs to another workspace. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "keeps workspace defaults isolated across multiple owner workspaces"`.

- 2026-05-14 timed combat condition expiry follow-up: the combat lifecycle API regression now advances a timed `restrained:2` combatant condition across multiple rounds, verifies it decrements to `restrained:1`, then expires while preserving the terminal `dead` rules state. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- --run src/app.test.ts -t "supports combat lifecycle"`.

- 2026-05-14 browser timed combat condition expiry follow-up: the browser combat tracker smoke now advances Valen's timed `prone:2` condition to the round-two `expires in 1 round` state, then advances to round three and verifies the visible condition-timing region reports `No timed conditions`. Validation passed: `pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/auth-tabletop.spec.ts -g "GM can run the browser combat tracker lifecycle"`.

- 2026-05-14 paired API permission-outcome follow-up: the OpenAPI-checked auth matrix now adds a first GM-positive permission slice for the explicit observer-negative fixtures: token creation/update, annotation creation, journal creation, chat send, dice roll, combat start, proposal creation, AI thread read/create, plugin install, system install, and actor roll. The expanded permission test and two existing long-running system/import integration tests now use explicit timeouts so the full API suite remains deterministic under full-suite load. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access|supports the D&D 5.5e SRD system as a first-class rules runtime|imports system characters from normalized character data"`; `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test`; `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`; `pnpm docs:site:check`; `git diff --check`.

- 2026-05-14 privileged read permission-outcome follow-up: the OpenAPI-checked auth matrix now adds a privileged GM/server-admin companion for every observer-forbidden protected `GET` route in the served user-session surface. It verifies organization and campaign invite rosters, fog/rendering diagnostics, dice macros, AI operation reads, plugin storage reads backed by an installed/configured plugin fixture, and server-admin operation reads clear the permission gate with explicit expected statuses while preserving the observer-denial assertions. Validation passed: `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test -- -t "rejects unauthenticated and unauthorized campaign access"`; `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api test`; `pnpm --filter @open-tabletop/api typecheck`; `pnpm docs:site:check`; `git diff --check`.
